owner: tools/localqaschedule, localqarunner, and local QA dashboard

observation.summary: Local QA evidence had daily scheduling and expires_at wording, but the dashboard and semantic binding could not prove that expired evidence is refreshed instead of merely displayed as stale.

hypothesis.summary: A schedule evidence field for expired-evidence refresh, plus dashboard coverage and semantic binding peers, can make the daily QA loop fail when freshness ownership drifts.

execution.summary: Emit refreshes_expired_evidence in schedule evidence, read freshness fields in the dashboard, and expose local.qa.daily_freshness as a coverage scenario.

evaluation.summary: Unit tests and local-qa-runner CI verify the generated schedule evidence, freshness failure mode, coverage row, and workflow grep evidence.

retrospective.summary: Expired evidence now has an owner loop: daily schedule evidence must prove it refreshes stale rows, and semantic binding blocks one-sided changes to that claim.

evidence.proves: schedule evidence and dashboard freshness coverage are tested together
public CI preserves refreshes_expired_evidence and local.qa.daily_freshness artifacts
